module_sme_rollup.name: Project Setup & Structure
module_sme_rollup.description: Create, organize, and template projects with clear scope, hierarchy, and traceability.

statements.text: Create project master records with core attributes, status lifecycle, and customer/contract linkage.
Organize projects into phases, milestones, and nested work breakdown structure with reusable templates.
Define project scope of work and apply custom fields and tags for business classification.
Organize projects into hierarchical programme structures with consolidated portfolio reporting.

module_sme_rollup.name: Task Execution & Tracking
module_sme_rollup.description: Assign, schedule, and monitor work with dependencies, progress visibility, and real-time dashboards.

statements.text: Create and assign tasks with title, description, assignee, priority, due date, and dependencies.
Define task dependencies with finish-to-start, start-to-start relationships and auto-calculate dates.
Organize work into task and subtask hierarchies with checklists for granular progress tracking.
Display interactive Gantt chart with drag-and-drop scheduling, critical path, and baseline overlay.
Provide Kanban board with configurable columns, swimlanes, WIP limits, and sprint planning.
Display real-time dashboard showing work progress, task completion metrics, and project health status.

module_sme_rollup.name: Resource & Cost Management
module_sme_rollup.description: Allocate resources, track time and expenses, and monitor budget versus actual spend.

statements.text: Assign team members to projects and tasks with specified effort hours and view capacity across all projects.
Exclude leave and public holidays from capacity calculations and display over/under-allocation indicators.
Log hours against projects and tasks, classify as billable or non-billable, and route through approval workflow.
Provide real-time timer and weekly grid view with daily totals, overtime flagging, and locked records post-approval.
Capture and approve expense claims by category with receipt upload, policy validation, and mileage calculation.
Define project budgets by labour, materials, travel, and subcontractor with real-time tracking and variance alerts.
Calculate labour costs automatically from timesheets using role and employee-specific rate cards.
Alert when actual spend exceeds configurable budget threshold and maintain revision history with audit trail.

module_sme_rollup.name: Billing, Revenue & Reporting
module_sme_rollup.description: Generate invoices, recognize revenue, and deliver comprehensive project performance analytics.

statements.text: Generate invoices from time-and-materials, fixed-price milestones, or progress-based billing methods.
Post invoices to accounts receivable and consolidate multiple projects into single customer invoice.
Generate unbilled time and expense reports for review before invoicing and manage retainer drawdowns.
Display project health with Red/Amber/Green status, milestone tracking, and cost variance analysis.
Report team utilisation, resource availability, and timesheet summaries by project, person, or period.
Calculate project profitability as revenue minus direct and indirect costs with margin analysis by type and client.
Export reports to PDF or CSV and automatically email on scheduled cadence to stakeholders.

module_sme_rollup.name: Risk, Change & Governance
module_sme_rollup.description: Manage risks and issues, control changes, and enforce approval workflows with audit trails.

statements.text: Record risks with probability, impact, risk score, owner, and mitigation plan linked to tasks or milestones.
Log issues with severity, owner, and resolution date; escalate high-severity issues automatically.
Capture change requests with impact analysis on budget, schedule, and resources through multi-level approval.
Automatically update project plan, budget, and schedule when change order is approved with full audit trail.
Configure multi-level approval chains with mandatory field validation and email notifications on state changes.
Escalate approvals when time thresholds exceeded and auto-assign tasks based on role, skill, or availability.

module_sme_rollup.name: Collaboration, Access & Integration
module_sme_rollup.description: Enable team collaboration, customer visibility, and system integration with security controls.

statements.text: Store, version, and control access to project files centrally with threaded comments and activity feed.
Send email notifications for document updates, mentions, and collaborative activity with real-time editing.
Provide customer portal with project progress, milestone status, deliverable sign-off, and change requests.
Implement role-based access control by project with unique login, two-factor authentication, and SSO support.
Maintain audit logs of user identity, timestamp, and actions; encrypt data at rest and in transit.
Import and export project data via CSV/Excel; integrate with accounting, calendar, and third-party systems via API.
Provide web and mobile access with responsive design; enable time logging and task updates from mobile devices.

Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your project data is accessible, secure, and connected to your other business systems. Singapore SMEs must comply with PDPA regulations and protect client information, while your team needs to access projects from the office, site, or home on any device. Without this foundation, you risk data breaches, compliance violations, and siloed information that slows decision-making.

Project Core.narrative: This family gives you the core tools to plan, assign, and track work—from breaking down a project into tasks to reminding the team of deadlines and keeping documents in one place. For a Singapore SME juggling multiple clients or internal projects, this prevents tasks from falling through the cracks and keeps everyone aligned on what's due and who's responsible. A construction firm using templates to standardise site handover checklists, or a consulting firm using Kanban to manage concurrent client engagements, both rely on these capabilities to stay organised.

Time & Resources.narrative: This family tracks who is working on what, how long it takes, and what it costs—critical for billing clients accurately and spotting when your team is overloaded. Singapore SMEs often bill by the hour or need to prove time spent to clients; without time tracking and resource visibility, you lose revenue and burn out staff. A digital agency tracking billable hours per client project, or a consulting firm balancing resource allocation across three concurrent engagements, both depend on this to stay profitable and sustainable.

Financial Control.narrative: This family keeps projects on budget and ensures you invoice correctly and on time—protecting your cash flow and profit margins. Singapore SMEs often operate on tight margins; if you don't track costs against budget or bill accurately, a single project can wipe out your quarter's profit. A renovation contractor managing material costs and labour against a fixed-price contract, or a software house billing milestone-based, both need these controls to avoid cost overruns and payment delays.

Delivery & Tracking.narrative: This family helps you see the big picture—what's on track, what's at risk, and what needs approval—so you can steer projects to success and keep clients informed. Singapore SMEs often manage projects with tight deadlines and demanding clients; without visibility into risks, dependencies, and progress, surprises emerge too late to fix. A project manager using a Gantt chart to spot a critical path delay before it hits the deadline, or a team using an issue register to escalate a supplier problem early, both avoid costly delays and client dissatisfaction.

Analytics & Reporting.narrative: This family turns project data into insights—showing which projects are profitable, which teams are stretched, and where processes can improve. Singapore SMEs need to make fast, data-driven decisions to compete; without dashboards and reports, you're flying blind on profitability and performance. A managing director reviewing monthly project profitability to decide which service lines to grow, or a finance manager spotting that one client type consistently runs over budget, both use these insights to steer the business.
